text: The impact of an AI code generator on learning introductory programming We investigate the impact of an AI code generator (OpenAI Codex) on novice programmers’ learning introductory programming. An experiment was run on 69 novice programmers (ages 10–17) who worked on 45 Python code-authoring tasks with or without the assistance of Codex, followed by a code-modification task for each task. We found that Codex significantly improved learners’ code-authoring performance (by 115% more completed code and by an average of 180% more correct tokens) and did so without negatively impacting learners’ performance on the code-modification tasks. Learners who received Codex assistance during the training phase performed slightly more successfully on the evaluation post-tests administered one week later, but not significantly so. Furthermore, we found that only those learners with higher Scratch pre-test scores benefited from receiving Codex assistance during the training phase, with a more successful retention of programming skills on the post-tests.
